section_header: GHG REDUCTION TARGETS / ACCOUNTABILITY AND IMPLEMENTATION
policy_statement: City of Chicago aims to reduce city GHG emissions by a minimum of 62% by 2040 (using 2017 inventory levels as baseline).
verbatim_text:
The 2022 CAP aims to chart an equitable path to reduce Chicago's GHG emissions by a minimum of 62% by 2040.

Guided by the strategies and actions of this plan, the City of Chicago aims to reduce the city's level of GHG emissions by 62% by 2040, based on 2017 GHG inventory levels.
extraction_rationale: Citywide quantified emissions reduction target with deadline explicitly stated.

section_header: Chicago Recovery Plan funding overlap with Chicago's 2022 Climate Action Plan
policy_statement: Plant 75,000 trees over 5 years (Chicago Recovery Plan climate investment: Expand Canopy Coverage, $46 million).
verbatim_text: Expand Canopy Coverage | $46 million | Plant 75,000 trees over 5 years in underserved community areas.
extraction_rationale: Funded program with a quantified deliverable and timeframe.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION
policy_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for transportation.
verbatim_text: Guided by the 2013 Climate Action Plan and a review of recent emissions trends and climate actions, we have identified a suite of near-term priority actions, which will help the City remain on track to meet our climate protection goals. The actions are presented below and summarized in Table 1.
extraction_rationale: Introduces a set of near-term priority actions followed by distinct action items under the TRANSPORTATION heading.

section_header: Improving mobility through pricing
policy_statement: Develop and release a strategy to address congestion and transportation emissions through pricing, coupled with investments in expanded transit and electrification in underserved communities.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for transportation.
verbatim_text: Announce that the City will develop and release a strategy to address congestion and transportation emissions through pricing, coupled with investments in expanded transit and electrification in underserved communities.
extraction_rationale: Specific planned intervention (pricing strategy) with linked investment focus; presented as an action item under the TRANSPORTATION near-term priorities.

section_header: Electric vehicle readiness ordinance for new construction
policy_statement: Pass a new electric vehicle readiness ordinance in 2018 to ensure new construction or renovation of parking structures is built with electric vehicle infrastructure.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for transportation.
verbatim_text: Pass a new electric vehicle readiness ordinance in 2018, which will ensure new construction or renovation of parking structures is built with electric vehicle infrastructure.
extraction_rationale: Clear commitment with a deadline (2018) to pass an ordinance.

section_header: Charging station network map & strategy
policy_statement: Release a map of optimal distribution of charging infrastructure in 2018 and use it to guide public and private investment with a focus on equitable distribution.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for transportation.
verbatim_text: Release map of optimal distribution of charging infrastructure in 2018. The map will guide public and private investment in the network, based on a set of criteria, with a specific focus on equitable distribution
extraction_rationale: Time-bound deliverable (2018 map) and specified intended use (guiding investment/equity).

section_header: Ride share and taxi fleet electrification
policy_statement: Work with stakeholders to create recommendations for electrifying shared/for-hire fleets, determine a year after which all such vehicles must be electric, and prepare to permit only electric vehicles for for-hire use.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for transportation.
verbatim_text: The city will work with these stakeholders to create recommendations on how to electrify shared vehicle fleets, determine a year after which all such vehicles must be electric, and prepare for the City to begin permitting only electric vehicles for for-hire use.
extraction_rationale: Specific regulatory preparation (permitting only EVs) and defined planning outputs; listed as a near-term action.

section_header: Green Fleet Action Plan update
policy_statement: Create a new Green Fleet Action Plan by the end of 2018 to accelerate electrification of the municipal fleet and phase out fossil fuel use in municipal vehicles.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for transportation.
verbatim_text: Create a new Green Fleet Action Plan, by the end of 2018, to accelerate the electrification of the municipal fleet and phase out fossil fuel use in municipal vehicles.
extraction_rationale: Clear time-bound commitment (end of 2018) to create a new plan with defined objectives.

section_header: GHG EMISSIONS ASSESSMENT
policy_statement: Near-term climate action priorities to assess GHG emissions impacts of City decisions.
verbatim_text: The City of Seattle does not current have a city-wide policy for tracking the carbon impacts of policies or projects, which limits our ability to fully understand the cost or savings related to carbon emissions.
extraction_rationale: This heading introduces a discrete action item immediately below; treated as a parent for the listed action.

section_header: Assess GHG Emissions Impact of City decisions
policy_statement: Issue an Executive Order directing City departments to assess the GHG emissions impact of City plans, policies, and major investments, with OSE to define scope and method by October 2018.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities to assess GHG emissions impacts of City decisions.
verbatim_text: Issue an Executive Order directing City departments to assess the GHG emissions impact of City plans, policies, and major investments. The Mayor will direct the Office of Sustainability & Environment to work with departments to define the types of projects to assess, the assessment method, and how to account for the broader costs of carbon, by October 2018.
extraction_rationale: Binding mechanism (Executive Order) plus a dated implementation milestone (October 2018).

section_header: BUILDINGS
policy_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for buildings.
verbatim_text: To accomplish these goals, policies will first aim to spur actions through incentives and other measures, as well as leading efforts in City-owned buildings, with specific requirements when needed.
extraction_rationale: BUILDINGS section presents multiple distinct actions underneath it (code, pilots, standards, utility programs, municipal targets, conversions), fitting a parent grouping.

section_header: Washington State tiered residential energy code
policy_statement: Pursue state legislation to enable a tiered residential energy code, making it a City priority in the 2019 legislative session.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for buildings.
verbatim_text: Legislation to allow for tiered energy codes will be a priority for the City of Seattle in the 2019 legislative session.
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to prioritize legislation in a specific session (2019).

section_header: Establish new 2030 Challenge pilot for 20 upgraded, high performing projects by 2025
policy_statement: Create a new pilot program offering additional height and floor area incentives for up to 20 major renovations in exchange for deep reductions including no fossil fuels for heating, and adjust related incentives/penalties consistent with the 2030 Challenge.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for buildings.
verbatim_text: A new pilot will offer additional height and floor area incentives for up to 20 major renovations in urban centers outside the International District. Projects would receive the incentives in exchange for cutting energy and water use well below code, including no use of fossil fuels for heating.
extraction_rationale: Specific pilot intervention with a numeric cap (20 projects) and defined performance exchange.

section_header: Incentive-driven 2030 performance standards for commercial and multifamily buildings
policy_statement: Provide programs and incentives to improve efficiency and reduce emissions in commercial and multifamily buildings, backed by a minimum performance standard beginning in 2030.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for buildings.
verbatim_text: Provide programs and incentives to spur improved energy efficiency and reduced carbon emissions, backed by a minimum performance standard beginning in 2030.
extraction_rationale: Defines a future binding mechanism (minimum performance standard) with a start date (2030) supported by interim programs.

section_header: City Light Whole Building Pay for Performance (P4P) Programs
policy_statement: Seattle City Light will pilot Energy Efficiency as a Service (EEaS) as part of whole-building programs to enable deeper energy efficiency in commercial buildings.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for buildings.
verbatim_text: Seattle City Light will pilot Energy Efficiency as a Service (EEaS), which is explicitly designed to help participants overcome the split incentive barrier in commercial buildings
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to pilot a named utility program (EEaS).

section_header: Improve municipal building energy efficiency and reduce carbon emissions by 2025
policy_statement: Double the City’s budget allocation from 2021–2025 and target an overall 40% energy and carbon emissions reduction in municipal buildings by 2025.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for buildings.
verbatim_text: Building on existing efforts to reduce energy use 20 percent by 2020, the City will double its budget allocation from 2021 to 2025, and set a new target to achieve an overall 40 percent energy and carbon emissions reduction in municipal buildings by 2025.
extraction_rationale: Resource allocation (double budget) plus quantified target and deadline (40% by 2025).

section_header: Oil to heat pump conversion
policy_statement: OSE will propose recommendations to accelerate converting 18,000 homes from oil heat to electric heat pumps, including financing for low-income residents and public/private incentives.
parent_statement: Near-term climate action priorities for buildings.
verbatim_text: OSE will propose recommendations to the mayor aimed at accelerating the transition of 18,000 homes from heating with oil to an electric heat pump, and adequately serving those unable to finance the switch alone. Recommendations will include a financing plan to support low-income residents, as well as a combination of public and private incentives to spur action.
extraction_rationale: Defined action with a quantified scale (18,000 homes) and specified program elements (financing/incentives).

section_header: Background
policy_statement: Seattle adopted a community goal to become carbon neutral by 2050 and directed creation of a plan to meet the goal (Resolution 31312).
verbatim_text: In 2011, the Mayor and City Council adopted a bold climate protection goal for our community to become carbon neutral by 2050 and directed the creation of a plan to meet the goal (Resolution 31312).
extraction_rationale: Formal city goal and directive via cited resolution; clear target year (2050) and governance action.

section_header: Municipal Buildings
policy_statement: Seattle adopted a Resource Conservation Management Plan with a goal to reduce energy use across City-owned buildings by 20% by 2020 (2008 baseline).
verbatim_text: In 2013, the City adopted a Resource Conservation Management Plan (Resolution 31491), with the goal to achieve a 20 percent reduction in energy use across City-owned buildings by 2020 (2008 baseline).
extraction_rationale: Formal plan with quantified target, deadline, and baseline.

section_header: LETTER FROM MAYOR DANIELLA LEVINE CAVA
policy_statement: Implement the Climate Action Strategy (CAS) to cut Miami-Dade communitywide greenhouse gas emissions 50% by 2030 from 2019 levels, with the goal of achieving net zero by 2050.
verbatim_text: Officially launched on Oct. 27, 2021, this plan describes work taking place on multiple fronts to cut MiamiDade's greenhouse gas emissions 50 percent by 2030 from 2019 levels, with the goal of achieving net zero by 2050.
extraction_rationale: Contains quantified emissions-reduction targets with deadlines (50% by 2030; net zero by 2050) tied to an implemented strategy.

section_header: NEW 2030 CLIMATE EQUITY GOALS FOR AUSTIN — Sustainable Buildings
policy_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Sustainable Buildings

- Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30%.
extraction_rationale: Quantified 2030 building-sector targets; followed by multiple numbered strategies in the Sustainable Buildings section.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
policy_statement: Ensure benefits of building decarbonization programs flow to low-income communities and communities of color through comprehensive energy poverty mitigation and partnerships.
parent_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Ensure benefits flow to low-income communities and communities of color

Pursue a comprehensive energy poverty mitigation strategy by partnering with trusted community organizations, affordable housing developers, and schools in equitable outreach and program development.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (energy poverty mitigation strategy and partnerships) tied to the buildings goal.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
policy_statement: Enhance resident and building owner understanding of energy consumption via outreach, culturally relevant communications, expanded benchmarking for existing buildings, and better access to energy and water data.
parent_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Enhance understanding of energy consumption

Enhance resident and building owner understanding of energy savings opportunities, benefits, and climate impacts of energy consumption. This will be done through direct outreach, culturally relevant communications, expanding benchmarking requirements for all existing buildings, and better access to energy and water data.
extraction_rationale: Concrete programmatic actions (benchmarking expansion; data access; outreach).

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
policy_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon building milestones through new building energy codes/amendments and incentives/education for existing building decarbonization, in collaboration with utilities and with equity focus.
parent_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Achieve energy-efficient, net-zero carbon buildings

Achieve goal milestones for net-zero carbon buildings through new building energy codes, amendments, and other methods. Engage owners and operators of existing buildings to decarbonize through incentives and education for contractors and occupants. Collaborate with local utilities to implement equitable emission reduction strategies.
extraction_rationale: Specific policy mechanisms (codes, amendments, incentives, education, utility collaboration).

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
policy_statement: Create equitable workforce development and training opportunities for emerging building-related technologies by partnering with local unions and education/advocacy organizations serving low-income communities and communities of color.
parent_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Ensure equitable workforce development for emerging technologies

Prioritize investment in local emissions reduction and create equitable workforce development and training opportunities for emerging technologies by partnering with local unions, education, and advocacy organizations that serve low-income communities and communities of color.
extraction_rationale: Defines a workforce development intervention with specified partners and equity focus.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Refrigerants
policy_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text: By 2030, reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25%.
extraction_rationale: Quantified emissions-reduction target with a deadline; followed by a list of strategies.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
policy_statement: Develop a refrigerant destruction program that places a price on older high ozone-depleting and high-GWP refrigerants to increase capture and safe destruction.
parent_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Capture and destroy old refrigerants

Develop a refrigerant destruction program that places a price on older high ozone-depleting substances and global warming potential (GWP) refrigerants.
extraction_rationale: Specific program intervention (destruction program with pricing mechanism).

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
policy_statement: Move forward with building code amendments and other local actions to allow/encourage low and no-GWP refrigerants as soon as feasible.
parent_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Improve building codes to encourage cleaner refrigerants

Closely follow developments in revised building codes that allow the use of low and no GWP refrigerants... and move forward with code amendments and other local action as soon as feasible.
extraction_rationale: Regulatory/code change commitment related to refrigerants.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
policy_statement: Create incentives for refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair in partnership with food retail, restaurants, warehouses, and HVAC companies, prioritizing outreach to small businesses owned by people of color.
parent_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Create incentives for leak detection and repair

Partner with grocery stores, convenience stores, restaurants... to create an incentive for designing and tracking refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair.
extraction_rationale: Concrete incentive-based policy with named partner sectors.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
policy_statement: Create an awareness, education, and training campaign for HVAC service providers and related stakeholders; lead by example with refrigerant leak detection/repair at City-owned facilities.
parent_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Awareness and training for HVAC service providers

Create an awareness, education, and training campaign... Lead by example by implementing a refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair program at City-owned facilities.
extraction_rationale: Specific campaign plus a City operational commitment (lead by example).

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
policy_statement: Reduce the volume/use of refrigerants, particularly high-GWP refrigerants, by expanding and prioritizing codes and incentives favoring passive design, reduction, and efficiency.
parent_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 5: Reduce the volume of refrigerants

Emphasize the link between design and refrigerant use by reducing and preventing the use of refrigerants... particularly those with high GWP.

How we'll get there:

- Ensure codes and incentives that favor passive design, reduction, and efficiency are expanded and prioritized.
extraction_rationale: Concrete policy direction to use codes/incentives to reduce refrigerant use.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Embodied Carbon
policy_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
verbatim_text:
By 2030, reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline*.

*The embodied carbon associated with the construction and materials must not exceed 500 kg CO2e/m2 (~100 lbs. CO2e/sf) per project.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target plus specific performance threshold; followed by multiple strategies.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
policy_statement: Develop City of Austin design and construction specifications and purchasing agreements to deliver healthy, low-carbon buildings, prioritizing community facilities serving low-income communities and communities of color first.
parent_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Lead by example through design and construction standards

In partnership with other cities and states, develop City of Austin design and construction specifications and purchasing agreements to result in healthy, low-carbon buildings.
extraction_rationale: Specific government procurement/standards action (lead by example).

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
policy_statement: Integrate lower-carbon building materials and deconstruction practices into City incentive programs (e.g., expedited permitting and Austin Energy Green Building) and transition voluntary guidance into planning/development agreements over time.
parent_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Incentivize lower-carbon materials

Enhance and integrate lower-carbon building materials and deconstruction practices into City incentive programs, like the expedited permitting process and Austin Energy's Green Building program, to transition voluntary design guidance into planning and development agreements over time.
extraction_rationale: Clear incentive/program modification commitment.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
policy_statement: Create a performance framework and educational programming on low-carbon materials best practices for industry professionals and the public, focusing on low-income communities and communities of color.
parent_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Educate stakeholders on materials best practices

Create a performance framework and educational programming for industry professionals and the general public, focusing on low-income communities and communities of color, to reduce the lifecycle and negative health impacts of building materials and construction practices.
extraction_rationale: Specific education/programming intervention tied to embodied carbon and health impacts.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
policy_statement: Prioritize partnerships within local materials markets to decarbonize high-impact building materials (e.g., glass, steel, aluminum, concrete, drywall, insulation, carpet).
parent_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Stimulate decarbonization with local producers installation

Prioritize partnerships within local materials markets to decarbonize high-impact materials, specifically glass, steel, aluminum, concrete, drywall, insulation, and carpet.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention focusing on decarbonizing named material categories via partnerships.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Water
policy_statement: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
verbatim_text: By 2030, equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
extraction_rationale: Quantified water-demand target with timeframe and implementation basis; followed by strategies.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Water demand ~152,000 acre-feet/year
policy_statement: Enhance community engagement and partnerships to advance Water Forward equity strategies (e.g., My ATX Water, incentives, ordinances) and implement targeted outreach to improve participation.
parent_statement: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Engage residents in water efficiency technological transitions, and conservation programs

Enhance community engagement strategies and create partnerships with community organizations to advance equity through Water Forward strategies, including My ATX Water (Advanced Metering Infrastructure), incentives, and ordinances.
extraction_rationale: Specific engagement and partnership actions to implement water conservation strategies with equity focus.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Water demand ~152,000 acre-feet/year
policy_statement: Evaluate water conservation and customer assistance program criteria to remove structural barriers and expand enrollment in the Customer Assistance Program and other low-income-serving programs.
parent_statement: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Evaluate water conservation, customer assistance, and workforce development program participation criteria

Evaluate program criteria to identify opportunities to address structural barriers that prevent program participation... explore strategies to expand enrollment in the Customer Assistance Program and increase participation in other programs serving low-income customers.
extraction_rationale: Concrete program evaluation and expansion commitment for equity in water assistance and conservation.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Water demand ~152,000 acre-feet/year
policy_statement: Assess and pursue water conservation and energy management optimization at the water-energy nexus, including evaluating Austin Water facility energy use and developing methods to quantify emissions reductions from water-demand reduction.
parent_statement: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Reduce emissions at the water-energy nexus

Assess how water demand reduction is associated with energy consumption in residential and commercial buildings and Austin Water facilities. Identify and pursue synergistic water conservation and energy management optimization efforts through programs and partnerships.
extraction_rationale: Specific assessment and optimization actions linking water conservation to emissions reductions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE
policy_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
verbatim_text: By 2030, 80% of new non-residential development is located within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
extraction_rationale: Quantified land-use development siting target with deadline; followed by multiple strategies.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
policy_statement: Adopt Complete Communities plans to reverse historical and racist impacts, address displacement, and preserve neighborhoods in communities of color.
parent_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Plan for complete communities

Adopt Complete Communities plans that reverse historical and racist impacts on areas experiencing displacement and preserve neighborhoods in communities of color.
extraction_rationale: Specific planning instrument commitment (adopt plans) tied to equity and land use.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
policy_statement: Work with medium and large employers to locate places of business in growth centers and along corridors.
parent_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Work with employers on location and amenities

Work with medium and large employers to locate their places of business in growth centers and along corridors.
extraction_rationale: Explicit intervention to influence employer location decisions toward target areas.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
policy_statement: Create community mobility hubs, including park-and-ride facilities, adjacent to transit stops with first/last-mile options.
parent_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3 : Create mobility hubs

Create community mobility hubs, including park-and-ride facilities, adjacent to transit stops to offer a complete trip experience. These hubs should offer various first- and last-mile mobility options.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure/program concept to improve multimodal access.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
policy_statement: Phase out free employee parking at City facilities and other large employers within 1/2 mile of the transit priority network, replacing it with cash-out, carpool/vanpool, transit passes, telework, or other commuter benefits.
parent_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Phase out free parking

Phase out the practice of providing free parking spaces to employees at City of Austin facilities and other large employers located within 1/2 mile of the Austin Strategic Mobility Plan's transit priority network. Instead, offer a parking cash-out, carpool and vanpool options, flex schedules, transit passes, support for teleworking, or other commuter benefits programs.
extraction_rationale: Specific policy change with defined scope and alternative measures.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE
policy_statement: Preserve and produce 135,000 housing units (including 60,000 affordable units) by 2027, with 75% of new housing located within 1/2 mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text: By 2027, preserve and produce 135,000 housing units, including 60,000 affordable housing units*, with 75% of new housing located within ½ mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
extraction_rationale: Quantified housing production/preservation target with timeframe and location share; followed by strategies.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 135,000 housing units by 2027
policy_statement: Engage communities vulnerable to displacement for immediate affordable housing assistance and proactively monitor at-risk affordable properties to extend affordability.
parent_statement: Preserve and produce 135,000 housing units (including 60,000 affordable units) by 2027, with 75% of new housing located within 1/2 mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Offer immediate affordable housing assistance

Engage directly with communities that are vulnerable to displacement and connect them with services. Proactively monitor affordable housing properties at risk of losing their affordability status to extend the period of affordability.
extraction_rationale: Direct programmatic commitment for anti-displacement and affordability preservation.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 135,000 housing units by 2027
policy_statement: Increase funding for the City's Housing Trust Fund and support capacity building for community development corporations.
parent_statement: Preserve and produce 135,000 housing units (including 60,000 affordable units) by 2027, with 75% of new housing located within 1/2 mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Fund affordable housing

Increase funding for the City's current Housing Trust Fund and support capacity building for community development corporations.
extraction_rationale: Resource allocation commitment (increase funding) and institutional capacity building.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 135,000 housing units by 2027
policy_statement: Include impacted low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities in Housing Investment Review Committee activities and in creating anti-displacement programs, and enhance culturally relevant outreach for subsidized affordable units in gentrifying areas.
parent_statement: Preserve and produce 135,000 housing units (including 60,000 affordable units) by 2027, with 75% of new housing located within 1/2 mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Enhance community engagement for affordable housing and antidisplacement programs

Include low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities directly affected by systemic inequalities in the City's Housing Investment Review Committee activities and when creating anti-displacement programs.
extraction_rationale: Governance/participation mechanism commitment for equitable housing program design and outreach.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE
policy_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text: By 2030, 50% of trips in Austin are made using public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided altogether by working from home*.
extraction_rationale: Quantified mode-shift target with deadline; followed by multiple strategies.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Work with the Austin Transit Partnership to implement Project Connect and expand and improve public transportation services.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Expand and improve public transportation

Work with the Austin Transit Partnership to implement Project Connect and expand and improve public transportation services.
extraction_rationale: Specific implementation commitment to deliver transit improvements through Project Connect.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Create comprehensive, user-friendly resources connecting community members with free transportation options and expand free transit options to increase access for low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Promote free transportation options

Create comprehensive, user-friendly resources connecting community members with free transportation options.

How we'll get there:

- Expand free transit options, including through Capital Metro, to provide increased transportation access for low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities.
extraction_rationale: Concrete access policy (resources + expanding free transit).

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Engage impacted communities to improve transit stops/stations and enforce ADA and accessibility guidelines around transit and public spaces.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Enhance transit stations and stops

Partner with Capital Metro, developers, and community organizers to engage low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities to improve transit stops, stations, and access to these facilities.

How we'll get there:

- Enforce the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and Public Rights-of-Way Accessibility Guidelines regulations...
extraction_rationale: Specific implementation and enforcement commitments for equitable, accessible transit facilities.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Implement 2020 Proposition B bonds for transportation infrastructure (sidewalks, bikeways, trails, safety, safe routes to school, substandard streets) in low-income communities and communities of color through meaningful engagement.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Prioritize bicycle networks

Implement the 2020 Proposition B voter-approved general obligation bonds for transportation infrastructure... in low-income communities and communities of color through a meaningful community engagement process.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure investment implementation tied to a named funding mechanism (Proposition B).

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Provide access to free or reduced-price bicycles and bicycle training for communities of color and train police officers on bicycle laws and racial profiling to improve safety and comfort for riders of color.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 5: Enhance bicycle education and training

Provide access to free or reduced-priced bicycles and basic bicycle training for communities of color and train police officers on bicycle laws and racial profiling to improve the comfort and safety of people of color riding bicycles.
extraction_rationale: Specific program/training interventions with equity and safety focus.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Update the City's Sidewalk Plan and Urban Trails Plan emphasizing equity and meaningful community engagement, and build priority segments while addressing ADA barriers and gaps.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 6: Improve sidewalks, urban trails, and crossings

Update the City's Sidewalk Plan and Urban Trails Plan, emphasizing equity and meaningful community engagement.

How we'll get there:

- Build all high- and very-high-priority sidewalk and trail segments and address ADA barriers and gaps...
extraction_rationale: Concrete planning update and build-out commitments with accessibility requirements.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Actively promote remote work, telework, and flexible commute policies, including a City telework policy encouraging at least 85% of eligible City employees to participate and supporting a goal of 15% residents working from home by 2039.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 7: Promote remote and flexible work policies

Actively promote remote work, telework, and flexible commute policies to maintain and encourage further reductions in single-occupancy vehicle trips.

How we'll get there:

- ...all new City of Austin telework and remote work policies contribute towards the goal of achieving 15% Austin residents working from home by 2039. This should include a strategy to implement a City of Austin telework policy encouraging no less than 85% of eligible City of Austin employees to participate in telework.
extraction_rationale: Specific policy approach with quantifiable participation targets/timeframe.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION
policy_statement: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).
verbatim_text: By 2030, 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in Austin are electrified, and electric vehicle ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse. This translates to approximately 460,000 electric vehicles on the road.
extraction_rationale: Quantified electrification target with timeframe and scale estimate; followed by strategies.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: 40% VMT electrified by 2030
policy_statement: Complete an Electric Vehicle Community Needs Assessment to inform an EV adoption growth plan, using community partners and hiring residents from the communities served.
parent_statement: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Conduct an EV Community Needs Assessment

Complete an Electric Vehicle Community Needs Assessment... The assessment will inform an EV adoption growth plan...
extraction_rationale: Specific planning/assessment commitment tied to equity and EV adoption.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: 40% VMT electrified by 2030
policy_statement: Create inclusive and easily accessible incentives for buying or leasing EVs, prioritizing low-income communities and communities of color and areas with limited transit access.
parent_statement: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).
verbatim_text:

parent_statement: Include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Reclaim public space and prioritize green infrastructure

Identify and reclaim mono-use, underused, and unconventional public spaces... with a focus on green infrastructure.

...A study should be conducted to examine the use of surface-level parking lots on City property to develop a plan to convert an ambitious percentage of these to functional green uses when feasible.
extraction_rationale: Concrete land-use/green infrastructure actions including a specified study and conversion planning.

section_header: NATURAL SYSTEMS — Goal: City-owned lands neutral/negative emissions management plans
policy_statement: Promote community stewardship of neighborhood public lands by establishing Neighborhood Stewardship Councils/Ambassadors, providing paid training/compensation, and creating grant assistance and staff positions to support excluded communities.
parent_statement: Include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Promote community stewardship

Promote community stewardship and management of neighborhood public lands to ensure higher levels of care and maintenance.

How we'll get there:

- Establish Neighborhood Stewardship Councils or Ambassadors...
extraction_rationale: Specific governance/program design including paid roles and funding support mechanisms.

section_header: NATURAL SYSTEMS — Goal: City-owned lands neutral/negative emissions management plans
policy_statement: Explore carbon farming via food forests and community gardens on public lands using low-carbon practices, including supporting local research in partnership with institutions and organizations serving low-income communities and communities of color.
parent_statement: Include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Promote carbon farming

Explore the ability of food forests and community gardens on public lands to use low-carbon and carbon farming practices.
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to explore and support carbon farming practices on public lands.

section_header: NEW EMISSIONS GOAL FOR AUSTIN
policy_statement: Austin will achieve net-zero community-wide greenhouse gas emissions by 2040.
verbatim_text: Austin will achieve net-zero community-wide greenhouse gas emissions by 2040.
extraction_rationale: A quantified, time-bound citywide emissions target (net-zero by 2040).

section_header: 8.1 Dakar's vision for 2050
policy_statement: By 2050, Dakar aims to achieve a 54% reduction in GHG emissions, a 75% reduction in the energy bill of the City’s built heritage, and at least a 65% share of renewable energy in the energy mix.
extraction_rationale: Quantified long-term targets with a clear timeframe (2050).

section_header: 9.3.3 Living environment and public hygiene
policy_statement: Dakar will pursue adaptation objectives including strengthening climate/coastal/urban pollution data networks; reducing vulnerable populations through awareness; reducing heatwave vulnerability; increasing protection initiatives; reducing air pollutants by 2030; increasing urban green spaces by 150 ha by 2030; expanding ecological urban agriculture by 2030; and increasing climate-resilient infrastructure.
verbatim_text:
Adaptation measures aim to strengthen the resilience of natural and human systems to climate risks and impacts. The objectives set are as follows:
- Strengthen networks for observing, measuring and collecting climate, coastal and urban pollution data.
- Reduce the number of people vulnerable to  the  effects  of  climate  change  through awareness-raising and communication.
-  Reduce the vulnerability of populations to heatwaves.
- Increase initiatives to protect populations against risks and disasters linked to extreme events and climate change.
- Reduce air pollutants by 2030 to improve air quality and lower the rate of respiratory disease.
- Increase urban green spaces by 150 ha in Dakar by 2030
- Increase areas of ecological urban agriculture  and  access  to  better  quality agricultural  products  by  2030  to  strengthen citizens' food security
- Increase the proportion of infrastructure adapted and resilient to climate risks.
extraction_rationale: List of explicit objectives, including quantified targets and deadlines (2030).

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas
policy_statement: Line of Action #1: Rural fire prevention through structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas to reduce biomass fuel, slow fire spread, increase forest resilience to fire, and ensure safety of people and property.
verbatim_text:
## Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas

| Main objectives: • Reducing biomass fuel • Reducing the speed at which fires spread • Increasing the forest's resilience to fire • Ensuring the safety of people and property
extraction_rationale: A named line of action with an objective and multiple concrete measures listed beneath it, fitting a parent policy.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas
policy_statement: Introduce discontinuity bands/patches to prevent rural fires, including (re)afforestation with fire-resistant species, controlling excessive natural regeneration after fire, creating agricultural mosaics, and creating grazing areas (including use of goats) to control spontaneous vegetation.
parent_statement: Line of Action #1: Rural fire prevention through structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas to reduce biomass fuel, slow fire spread, increase forest resilience to fire, and ensure safety of people and property.
verbatim_text:
• Introduction of discontinuity bands or patches by:
- (re)afforestation with other forest species resistant to the risk of fire
- control of excessive densities of natural regeneration after fire
- introduction of agricultural mosaic
- creation of areas for grazing and use of goats to control spontaneous vegetation
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention measures listed under the named Line of Action #1.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas
policy_statement: Economically valorise biomass by installing intermunicipal systems for collecting and interim storage of biomass residues from forestry and agricultural activities in rural areas.
parent_statement: Line of Action #1: Rural fire prevention through structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas to reduce biomass fuel, slow fire spread, increase forest resilience to fire, and ensure safety of people and property.
verbatim_text: • Economic valorisation of biomass through the installation of Intermunicipal Systems for the collection and interim storage of biomass left over from forestry and agricultural activities in rural areas
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure/programmatic action under Line of Action #1.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas
policy_statement: Convert and reconfigure electricity transmission/distribution structures that cross forest areas (e.g., burying cables or changing routes) to reduce rural fire risk.
parent_statement: Line of Action #1: Rural fire prevention through structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas to reduce biomass fuel, slow fire spread, increase forest resilience to fire, and ensure safety of people and property.
verbatim_text: • Conversion and reconfiguration of linear electricity transmission and distribution structures that cross-forest areas (e.g. burying cables; changing routes)
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (grid infrastructure changes) under the Line of Action #1 list of measures.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas
policy_statement: Reconfigure telecommunications networks in rural/forest areas as part of rural fire prevention structural interventions.
parent_statement: Line of Action #1: Rural fire prevention through structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas to reduce biomass fuel, slow fire spread, increase forest resilience to fire, and ensure safety of people and property.
verbatim_text: • Reconversion and reconfiguration of telecommunications networks in areas
extraction_rationale: Concrete intervention listed as a measure under Line of Action #1.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas
policy_statement: Install and reconfigure warning, cut-off, traffic diversion, and local population surveillance/warning communication systems and signage for rural fire prevention.
parent_statement: Line of Action #1: Rural fire prevention through structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas to reduce biomass fuel, slow fire spread, increase forest resilience to fire, and ensure safety of people and property.
verbatim_text: • Installation and reconfiguration of warning, cut-off and traffic diversion • Installation of communication/information systems, including surveillance and warning systems for local populations and appropriate signage

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #2 - Implementation of soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques
policy_statement: Line of Action #2: Implement soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques to minimise soil erosion, increase soil organic matter and water retention, reduce runoff, and increase soil biodiversity and ecosystem resilience.
verbatim_text:
## Line of Action #2 - Implementation of soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques

| Main objectives:
| • Minimising soil erosion
| • Increase the levels of organic matter in the soil
| • Improve the soil's water retention capacity
| • Reduce runoff from extreme rainfall events
| • Increasing biodiversity in soils
extraction_rationale: Named line of action with multiple specific measures listed (measures/concretization).

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #2 - Implementation of soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques
policy_statement: Recover areas of degraded soils and vegetation, prioritising areas susceptible to desertification, including areas affected by rural/forest fires, eroded/salted/degraded areas, areas with invasive exotic woody species, over-exploited soils, and inert extraction areas.
parent_statement: Line of Action #2: Implement soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques to minimise soil erosion, increase soil organic matter and water retention, reduce runoff, and increase soil biodiversity and ecosystem resilience.
verbatim_text: Recovering areas of degraded soils and vegetation, with priority in the areas most susceptible to desertification, namely: i) areas affected by rural fires, including forest fires; ii) eroded, salted, and other degraded areas; iii) areas affected by invasive exotic woody species; iv) areas with soils degraded by over- exploitation...; and v) areas of inert extraction
extraction_rationale: Concrete restoration action listed under Measures/concretization for Line of Action #2.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #2 - Implementation of soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques
policy_statement: Apply soil conservation practices including afforestation with soil-improving species, incorporation of manures/compost and rotations with legumes/cover crops/by-products, direct seeding or in-line tillage and crop rotations, intercropping, and setting up overgrazing and biodiverse pastures.
parent_statement: Line of Action #2: Implement soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques to minimise soil erosion, increase soil organic matter and water retention, reduce runoff, and increase soil biodiversity and ecosystem resilience.
verbatim_text:
Measures/concretization:
• Afforestation with soil-improving species - long revolutions
• Incorporation of manures and compost, rotations with legumes, cover and with agricultural or forestry by-products...
• Direct seeding or in-line tillage; crop rotation with different types of systems
• Intercropping in permanent crops
• Setting up overgrazing and biodiverse pastures
extraction_rationale: Specific list of techniques to be implemented; directly stated measures.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #2 - Implementation of soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques
policy_statement: Reposition/rehabilitate riparian galleries (planting/sowing indigenous species) and consolidate/recover slopes and riverbanks using natural engineering techniques for erosion control.
parent_statement: Line of Action #2: Implement soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques to minimise soil erosion, increase soil organic matter and water retention, reduce runoff, and increase soil biodiversity and ecosystem resilience.
verbatim_text:
• Reposition/rehabilitation of the riparian gallery (planting and/or sowing of indigenous species)
• Consolidation and recovery of slopes and riverbanks using natural engineering techniques
extraction_rationale: Concrete nature-based interventions listed under Line of Action #2 measures.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #2 - Implementation of soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques
policy_statement: Create infiltration gardens and other aquifer recharge solutions (e.g., trenches and infiltration channels) as part of soil conservation and fertility improvement.
parent_statement: Line of Action #2: Implement soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques to minimise soil erosion, increase soil organic matter and water retention, reduce runoff, and increase soil biodiversity and ecosystem resilience.
verbatim_text: Creation of infiltration gardens and other aquifer recharge solutions (e. trenches and infiltration channels)
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (aquifer recharge solutions) explicitly listed.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #3 - Implementation of good water management practices...
policy_statement: Line of Action #3: Implement good water management practices in agriculture, aquaculture, industry and the urban sector to prevent impacts from drought and water scarcity, including reducing water consumption and increasing water efficiency and infrastructure resilience.
verbatim_text:
## Line of Action #3 - Implementation of good water management practices in agriculture, aquaculture, industry and the urban sector to prevent impacts resulting from drought and scarcity phenomena

| Main objectives: • Reduce water consumption in the various sectors • Increasing water efficiency in the various sectors ... • Increasing the resilience of water supply infrastructure
extraction_rationale: Named action with multiple concrete measures listed under Measures/concretization.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #3 - Implementation of good water management practices...
policy_statement: Adopt water-saving practices in agriculture, including efficient irrigation (sprinkler/localised systems and meters), monitoring crop water needs, remodelling irrigation infrastructure to reduce losses and evaporation, and using rainwater and reuse of treated wastewater.
parent_statement: Line of Action #3: Implement good water management practices in agriculture, aquaculture, industry and the urban sector to prevent impacts from drought and water scarcity, including reducing water consumption and increasing water efficiency and infrastructure resilience.
verbatim_text:
Measures/concretization: • Adoption of good water management practices in agriculture to reduce consumption:
- Irrigation practices that promote the more efficient and sustainable use of water resources [installation of sprinkler irrigation systems, localised (micro-sprinklers, drop-by-drop) and installation of meters at the water catchments]...
- Improved systems for monitoring the actual water needs of crops...
- Remodelling of irrigation infrastructures to reduce losses...
- Use of rainwater and re-use of treated wastewater in agriculture
extraction_rationale: Specific interventions enumerated under Line of Action #3.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #3 - Implementation of good water management practices...
policy_statement: Promote installation of species/varieties better adapted to climate change, including reconversion of crops to less water-demanding species/varieties/cultivars with high water productivity and resistance to water stress.
parent_statement: Line of Action #3: Implement good water management practices in agriculture, aquaculture, industry and the urban sector to prevent impacts from drought and water scarcity, including reducing water consumption and increasing water efficiency and infrastructure resilience.
verbatim_text:
- Installation of species and varieties better adapted to changes in climate and extreme events:
- - Reconversion of crops to less water-demanding species, varieties, and cultivars (adapting crops and varieties to water availability, favouring the installation of species with high water productivity and resistance to water stress)
extraction_rationale: Concrete adaptation action (crop reconversion) explicitly listed.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #3 - Implementation of good water management practices...
policy_statement: Adopt water management practices in industry to reduce consumption, including wastewater reuse and installation of rainwater harvesting systems.
parent_statement: Line of Action #3: Implement good water management practices in agriculture, aquaculture, industry and the urban sector to prevent impacts from drought and water scarcity, including reducing water consumption and increasing water efficiency and infrastructure resilience.
verbatim_text:
• Adoption of good water management practices in the industry to reduce consumption:
- Wastewater reuse in industry
- Installation of rainwater harvesting systems
extraction_rationale: Specific industrial water efficiency interventions listed under Line of Action #3.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #3 - Implementation of good water management practices...
policy_statement: Remodel aquaculture infrastructures to optimise water consumption and reduce losses.
parent_statement: Line of Action #3: Implement good water management practices in agriculture, aquaculture, industry and the urban sector to prevent impacts from drought and water scarcity, including reducing water consumption and increasing water efficiency and infrastructure resilience.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #4 - Increasing the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to the effects of climate change
policy_statement: Conserve and rehabilitate watercourses to maintain environmental flows and river continuity in endemic species distribution areas; regulate irrigation abstractions from catchments serving as summer refuges; install/adapt fish transposition devices on weirs.
parent_statement: Line of Action #4: Increase the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to climate change through measures such as riparian gallery interventions, maintaining environmental flows and river continuity, and creating refuges/ecological corridors for vulnerable species.
verbatim_text:
• Conservation and rehabilitation of watercourses to maintain environmental flows and the river continuity in the distribution areas of endemic species...
• Regulating the volumes of water abstracted for irrigation from catchments that serve as summer refuges for vulnerable freshwater and migratory fish species.
• Installation or adaptation of transposition devices on weirs for vulnerable freshwater and migratory fish.
extraction_rationale: Concrete ecosystem/water management actions explicitly listed as measures.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #4 - Increasing the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to the effects of climate change

Conservation of temporary pond systems: i) sites with important populations of the striped terrapin ... and the Mediterranean terrapin ...; and ii) preferably oriented N -> S in open areas, away from roads and maintaining 10-70% forest cover
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure/ecological connectivity measures under the line of action.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #4 - Increasing the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to the effects of climate change

extraction_rationale: Explicit habitat/corridor creation measures listed.
